data.title: Biggest stock movers Thursday: BABA, WMT, PLTR, CVNA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged down on Thursday morning as investors digested the minutes from the Federal Reserve’s latest policy meeting, which highlighted concerns that Trump’s policy proposals, particularly tariffs, could reignite inflation in the U.S.

Here are some of Thursday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Bausch Health (NYSE:BHC) surged over 8% after reporting better-than-expected Q4 results, with a 6% rise in reported revenue and a 9% increase on an organic basis. The company’s 2025 guidance projects revenue between $9.9B and $10.15B, with a midpoint of $10.03B, exceeding the consensus estimate of $9.91B. Adjusted EBITDA is expected to range from $3.53B to $3.68B, while adjusted cash flow from operations is forecasted between $975M and $1.025B.
  • Herbalife (NYSE:HLF) shares jumped 18% after delivering strong Q4 results and announcing a leadership transition, with President Stephan Gratziani set to become CEO on May 1, 2025, while current CEO Michael Johnson will move to Executive Chairman. The company’s outlook projects Q1 net sales declining 1.5% to 5.5% Y/Y, though constant currency sales could be flat to up 4%, with adjusted EBITDA expected between $140M and $150M. For FY2025, net sales are forecasted to range from a 3% decline to a 3% increase, while constant currency sales could rise 1% to 7%, and adjusted EBITDA is expected to be between $600M and $640M.
  • Chinese retailer Alibaba Group Holdings (NYSE:BABA) soared 10% after strong FQ3 earnings, beating expectations across key metrics. The e-commerce giant reported an adjusted net profit of 51.07B yuan ($7.01B), up 6% Y/Y, surpassing estimates. Revenue rose 8%, and adjusted EBITA grew 4% Y/Y to $7.52B. The cloud intelligence segment expanded 13.1%, while International Digital Commerce Group revenue soared 32% Y/Y, significantly outpacing expectations.

Biggest stock losers

  • Palantir Technologies (NASDAQ:PLTR) fell over 5% after a memo from Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, obtained by the Washington Post, revealed plans for an 8% annual cut to the defense budget over the next five years. The proposed reductions, due by Feb. 24, raised concerns about potential impacts on defense contracts. However, 17 categories are reportedly exempt, including border security, nuclear weapons modernization, missile defense, and one-way attack drones.
  • Shares of Carvana (NYSE:CVNA) dropped around 10% despite a strong Q4 performance, which saw the company beat top- and bottom-line estimates, increase car sales, and improve profit per vehicle. While Carvana expects “significant growth” in retail units sold and adjusted EBITDA in FY2025, with a sequential increase in both metrics in Q1, investor concerns may have been driven by its Form S-3 filing with the SEC, allowing for the potential sale of an undisclosed number of shares. The company’s long-term targets include gross profit growth of 15%-19% and an EBITDA margin of 8%-13.5%.
  • Retail giant Walmart (NYSE:WMT) saw its shares tumble 7% as its profit and sales outlook fell short of estimates, despite exceeding Q4 expectations. For 1QFY26, the company expects net sales growth of 3.0% to 4.0% (midpoint 3.5%), below the 3.86% consensus, while adjusted EPS is forecasted between $0.57 and $0.58 (midpoint $0.575), missing the $0.65 consensus. For FY2026, Walmart projects net sales growth of 3.0% to 4.0% (midpoint 3.5%), trailing the 4.19% consensus, with adjusted EPS guidance of $2.50 to $2.60 (midpoint $2.55), compared to the $2.77 consensus. The weaker-than-expected outlook highlights ongoing competitive pressures and potential margin constraints.

data.title: Laureate Education beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Laureate Education press release (NASDAQ:LAUR): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.62 beats by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $423.4M (+3.4% Y/Y) beats by $11.9M.
  • On an organic constant currency basis1, revenue increased by 10%.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $141.1 million, compared to $131.3 million for the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • New enrollments increased 5%.
  • Total enrollments increased 5%.

  • Outlook for Fiscal 2025

    Laureate's 2025 outlook shows continued growth opportunities on a constant currency basis. On an as-reported basis, based on current spot FX rates, we do expect an unfavorable translation impact from currency versus 2024 due to the weakening of the Mexican Peso in the second half of 2024.

    Based on the current foreign exchange spot rates2, Laureate currently expects its full-year 2025 results to be as follows:

    • Total enrollments expected to be in the range of 489,000 to 495,000 students, reflecting growth of 4%-5% versus 2024;
    • Revenues expected to be in the range of $1,545 million to $1,570 million vs. $1.61B consensus, reflecting flat performance to a slight decline of (1%) on an as-reported basis and growth of 6%-7% on an organic constant currency basis versus 2024, or 7%-8% growth excluding the impact from campus consolidations; and
    • Adjusted EBITDA expected to be in the range of $467 million to $477 million, reflecting growth of 4%-6% on an as-reported basis and 11%-13% on an organic constant currency basis versus 2024.

data.title: Alight appoints new board chair
data.description:
• Alight (NYSE:ALIT) board appointed Russell P. Fradin as its chairman of the board, succeeding William P. Foley, II, who will continue to serve as a board member, effective March 1, 2025.
  • Robert Schriesheim, Robert Lopes, Jr., and Mike Hayes have been appointed to the board, replacing Erika Meinhardt, Regina Paolillo and Dan Henson, effective as of March 1, 2025.
  • Schriesheim will serve on the audit and compensation committees, Lopes will serve on the audit and nominating and corporate governance committees and Hayes will serve on the audit and compensation committees.
  • ALIT shares rise 2.4% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: FTI Consulting misses top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• FTI Consulting press release (NYSE:FCN): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.56 misses by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $894.9M (-3.2% Y/Y) misses by $18.8M.
  • Introduces 2025 Guidance: 2025 Guidance:The Company estimates that revenues for full year 2025 will range between $3.660 billion and $3.810 billion vs. $3.90B consensus. The Company estimates that EPS for full year 2025 will range between $7.44 and $8.24 and Adjusted EPS will range between $7.80 and $8.60 vs. $8.68 consensus. The variance between EPS and Adjusted EPS guidance is related to the aforementioned first quarter of 2025 special charge, which the Company estimates will be approximately $17 million, or $0.36 per share.

data.title: HNI Corporation reports mixed Q4 results; initiates FY25 soft outlook
data.description:
• HNI Corporation press release (NYSE:HNI): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.87 beats by $0.13.
  • Revenue of $642.5M (-5.5% Y/Y) misses by $14.53M.
  • Elevated EPS visibility through 2026 from initiatives already underway.
  • Net sales growth anticipated to return in 2025 in both segments.

  • Outlook

    • First quarter demand environment. The Corporation expects first quarter 2025 net sales in Workplace Furnishings to decrease at a low-to-mid single-digit rate year-over-year. In Residential Building Products, first quarter 2025 net sales are expected to increase mid-single digits compared to the same period in 2024.
    • First quarter non-GAAP earnings per share. When including the anticipated impact of tariffs, non-GAAP earnings per share in the first quarter of 2025 are expected to decrease slightly from 2024 levels. Excluding a temporary drag from tariffs, the Corporation would have expected first quarter non-GAAP earnings per share to increase modestly driven by increasing productivity, partially offset by investments.
    • 2025 net sales outlook. For the full year 2025, the Corporation expects year-over-year net sales growth in the low-to-mid single digits in both segments vs. estimated growth of 2.73% Y/Y. In Workplace Furnishings, the benefits of improving pre-order, order, and backlog metrics are expected to drive net sales growth beginning in the second quarter, with year-over-year growth rates improving as the year progresses. In Residential Building Products, the majority of the year-over-year growth is expected late in 2025, as comparisons ease.
    • Double-digit EPS growth in 2025 vs. $3.48 consensus, and elevated earnings growth visibility through 2026. The Corporation expects another year of double-digit EPS growth in 2025, driven by KII synergies, the ramp-up of the Mexico facility, and increased profits from net sales growth. The remaining $0.70 to $0.80 of incremental EPS benefit from KII and Mexico are expected to be evenly distributed between 2025 and 2026, providing strong earnings visibility over the next two years. For the full year, the Corporation expects to fully offset any tariff-driven pressures.

data.title: Nanosonics targets 11%-14% revenue growth for FY 2025 driven by North American expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Nanosonics Limited (OTCPK:NNCSF) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Michael Kavanagh highlighted that the company achieved revenue of AUD 93.6 million for the first half, marking an 18% increase compared to the prior corresponding period and a 4% sequential growth over H2 FY2024. This performance prompted an upward revision in full-year revenue growth guidance to 11%-14%, assuming an FX rate of $0.67.
  • North American operations remain a strong growth driver, with the installed base growing by 940 units in the first half, reaching over 31,000 units. Trophon continues to consolidate its position as the standard of care in the region.
  • The company saw improvements in annuity revenue, driven by customer value expansion initiatives, including consumables, ecosystem products, and technical service contracts. Consumable and service revenue in North America grew 19% during the half.
  • European revenue increased 37% year-over-year to AUD 5.9 million, although new installed base growth was slower than anticipated. Management expects stronger performance in H2.
  • CFO Jason Burriss reported that gross profit margin improved to 78.5%, up from 76.3% in H2 FY2024, due to normalization of production volumes and inventory reduction efforts.
  • The Trophon-only business delivered AUD 25.6 million in pre-tax profit, reflecting growth in operating leverage, with PBT as a percentage of revenue rising to 27%.

Outlook

  • Revenue guidance for FY2025 was revised upward to 11%-14%, or 13%-16% if FX rates remain at $0.64 for H2.
  • Gross margin guidance was narrowed to 78%-79%, with expectations to maintain a level around 78.5%.
  • Management anticipates new installed base growth in North America in H2 to mirror H1 levels, with continued opportunities in customer value expansion.
  • In Europe, stronger new installed base performance is forecasted in H2, supported by an active pipeline and prioritizing markets with strong adoption fundamentals.
  • The CORIS system is expected to launch in Q1 FY2026, pending FDA de novo clearance. Marketing in Europe is not contingent on the FDA approval timeline.

Financial Results

  • Total revenue reached AUD 93.6 million, an 18% year-over-year increase. Capital revenue grew 11% to AUD 22.7 million, supported by a total of 1,730 units sold.
  • Consumables and service revenue surged by 20% to AUD 62 million, driven by higher installed base, increased ultrasound procedures, and customer value expansion activities.
  • Operating profit before tax was AUD 10.9 million, up 124% from AUD 4.9 million in the prior period, bolstered by disciplined cost controls and revenue growth.
  • Net profit for the half stood at AUD 9.8 million, a 58% increase year-over-year.
  • The company generated AUD 13.8 million in cash flow during the half, resulting in a cash balance of AUD 144.5 million with no debt.

Q&A

  • Josh Kannourakis, Barrenjoey: Asked about service revenue growth and its runway. CEO Kavanagh explained that service revenue grew over 20%, driven by increasing service contracts and upgrades. He emphasized that the majority of upgrade customers switch service providers from GE Healthcare to Nanosonics.
  • Shane Storey, Wilsons Advisory: Inquired about pricing dynamics in capital sales. CEO Kavanagh stated that pricing remained stable, with observed increases attributed to sales mix changes.
  • Elyse Shapiro, Canaccord Genuity: Asked about margin improvements from US manufacturing. CFO Burriss noted that margins on consumables are expected to improve incrementally, supported by AUD 3 million in CapEx for the Indianapolis facility.
  • Richard Hemming, Under the Radar Report: Queried the lack of dividends. CEO Kavanagh stated that the board prioritizes maintaining financial resilience and optionality for growth initiatives, including potential acquisitions and CORIS commercialization.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts exhibited a slightly positive tone, with questions focused on growth drivers, service revenue opportunities, and operating expenses. They sought clarity on long-term scalability and the CORIS approval timeline.
  • Management maintained a confident tone during both prepared remarks and analyst exchanges. CEO Kavanagh repeatedly emphasized growth opportunities and operational resilience, stating, "We expect similar new IB numbers in the second half to the first half."

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • No previous quarter transcript was available for comparison.
  • Management's tone reflected confidence in North American market expansion and annuity revenue growth.
  • Analysts concentrated on service revenue potential, CORIS commercialization, and cost management, aligning with management's strategic focus.

Risks and Concerns

  • Regulatory approval for CORIS remains a key dependency for its commercialization timeline. Management continues preparations for supply chain and manufacturing readiness.
  • Slower new installed base growth in Europe during H1 poses a regional risk, though management anticipates improvement in H2 based on pipeline activity.
  • Inflationary pressures on staffing and sales-related costs were noted as contributors to operating expense growth.

Final Takeaway

Nanosonics reported strong performance in Q2 2025, driven by North American market expansion and robust annuity revenue growth. With a revised revenue guidance range of 11%-14% for FY2025 and continued opportunities in customer value expansion, the company remains optimistic about its growth trajectory. CORIS commercialization and regional market dynamics, particularly in Europe and Asia, are key variables for future performance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Alight Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.02, revenue of $680M beats by $5.2M
data.description:
• Alight press release (NYSE:ALIT): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.24 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $680M (-0.3% Y/Y) beats by $5.2M.
  • Revenue decreased 0.3% to $680 million, as compared to $682 million in the prior year period. The change was due to lower project revenue, partially offset by higher net commercial activity. Recurring revenues were 90.7% of total revenue.
  • Business Process as a Service (BPaaS) revenue grew 9.8% to $146 million, representing 21.5% of total revenue

  • The Company's 2025 outlook includes:

    • Revenue of $2,318 million to $2,388 million vs $2.40B consensus
    • Adjusted EBITDA of $620 million to $645 million.
    • Adjusted diluted EPS of $0.58 to $0.64 vs $0.63 consensus
    • Free cash flow of $250 million to $285 million.

data.title: Anglo American writes down De Beers value by another $2.9B but analysts see progress
data.description:
Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said Thursday it wrote down the value of its struggling De Beers diamond business by another $2.9B, after already writing down the business by $1.6B to $7.6B last year.

The latest writeoff contributed to Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) booking a total of $3.8B in net impairments for 2024, taking the company to a wider loss than analysts had expected.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) reported a net loss of $3.07B for the full year, far above the $116.9M loss forecast by analysts, according to a consensus compiled by Visible Alpha, after posting a net profit of $283M in 2023, while full-year underlying EBITDA, the company's preferred profit metric, fell to $8.46B from $9.96B, missing the $8.76B analyst consensus estimate.

The annual decline reflected a 10% drop in average prices of its commodities, Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) said, but noted cost cutting helped deliver $1.3B in savings with another $500M expected by the end of 2025.

But the market welcomed the company's progress towards asset sales and reduced debt, sending shares +4.6% in London trading.

Anglo (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) "reduced the book value of De Beers by $2.9B to bring the carrying value of this business to a more reasonable $4.1B," Jefferies analysts said, also praising the company's operational improvements and progress on restructuring.

The $2.9B De Beers writedown was in-line with expectations, according to RBC Capital's Ben Davis and Marina Calero, and the miner made a successful step forward by agreeing to a deal with Chilean state copper company Codelco to implement a joint mine plan between Los Bronces and Andina, which will deliver straightforward synergies.

data.title: Endava Non-GAAP EPS of £0.30, revenue of £195.6M; initiates Q3 and FY25 outlook
data.description:
• Endava press release (NYSE:DAVA): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of £0.30.
  • Revenue of £195.6M (+6.5% Y/Y).
  • Endava announces $100 million share repurchase program.
  • Net cash from operating activities was £32.0 million in Q2 FY2025, compared to net cash from operating activities of £35.0 million in the same period in the prior year.
  • Adjusted free cash flow was £31.6 million in Q2 FY2025, compared to £33.6 million in the same period in the prior year.
  • At December 31, 2024, Endava had cash and cash equivalents of £60.1 million, compared to £62.4 million at June 30, 2024.

  • OUTLOOK:

    Third Quarter Fiscal Year 2025:

    Endava expects revenue will be in the range of £198.0 million to £200.0 million, representing a constant currency revenue increase of between 13.0% and 14.0% on a year over year basis. Endava expects adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of £0.31 to £0.32 per share.

    Full Fiscal Year 2025:

    Endava expects revenue will be in the range of £795.0 million to £800.0 million, representing a constant currency revenue increase of between 8.5% and 9.0% on a year over year basis. Endava expects adjusted diluted EPS to be in the range of £1.20 to £1.23 per share.
